# Stack — cheat sheet

<!-- Generated by `go generate ./...` (tools/docgen). Do not edit by hand. -->

## Commands

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `stack adopt <pr-number>` | Reconstruct a teammate's stack from a PR's embedded state |
| `stack remove <branch>` | Untrack a branch (grafting children onto its parent); optionally close its PR |
| `stack reparent <branch> --onto <new-parent>` | Move a branch onto a different parent and rebase it |
| `stack start <name>` | Create a branch on top of the current branch and track it in the stack |
| `stack submit` | Push the stack, open/refresh PRs, and publish state into each PR |
| `stack sync` | Cascade-rebase every branch onto its updated parent |

## Key flags

**`adopt`** — `--owner`, `--remote`, `--repo`

**`remove`** — `--close-pr`, `--delete-branch`, `--owner`, `--remote`, `--repo`, `--trunk`

**`reparent`** — `--onto`, `--trunk`

**`start`** — `--parent`, `--trunk`

**`submit`** — `--draft`, `--owner`, `--remote`, `--repo`, `--trunk`, `--update-titles`

**`sync`** — `--trunk`


## Common workflows

**Start a stack**
```sh
git checkout main
stack start feature-a     # branch off the trunk
# ...commit work...
stack start feature-b     # branch off feature-a (current branch)
# ...commit work...
```

**Open / refresh PRs**
```sh
export STACK_TOKEN=$(gh auth token)
stack submit --owner ORG --repo REPO
```

**Restack after the parent changes**
```sh
# amend or add commits to feature-a, then:
stack sync                # cascade-rebase children onto the new tips
stack submit --owner ORG --repo REPO
```

**Adopt a teammate's stack**
```sh
stack adopt 123 --owner ORG --repo REPO   # reconstruct from PR #123
stack sync
```

**Move a branch to a new parent**
```sh
stack reparent feature-b --onto feature-c
stack sync
```

**Drop a branch from the stack**
```sh
stack remove feature-b --close-pr --delete-branch --owner ORG --repo REPO
```
